Title: Egyptian Wisdom Literature (e.g., Instructions of Ptahhotep)
Content Summary: Advice from a "father" to a "son" on moral and spiritual wisdom.
Influence: Influenced format and themes of later Hermetic texts.

Title: Book of the Dead
Content Summary: Funerary texts with spells to guide the deceased through the afterlife.
Influence: Concepts echoed in later Hermetic ideas of spiritual ascent.

Title: Orphic Hymns and Teachings
Content Summary: Mystical poems and religious doctrines attributed to the mythical Orpheus.
Influence: Influenced later Hermetic and Neoplatonic thought.

Title: Pythagorean Teachings
Content Summary: Philosophical and mystical doctrines of Pythagoras and his followers.
Influence: Concepts integrated into later Hermetic philosophy.

Title: Plato's Dialogues (e.g., Timaeus, Phaedrus)
Content Summary: Philosophical discussions on the nature of reality, the soul, and knowledge.
Influence: Major influence on Hermetic cosmology and psychology.

Title: Stoic Philosophical Texts
Content Summary: Teachings on ethics, logic, and natural philosophy.
Influence: Concepts incorporated into Hermetic worldview.

Title: Dead Sea Scrolls
Content Summary: Religious texts of the Essenes, including apocalyptic and wisdom literature.
Influence: Parallels with some Hermetic concepts of divine knowledge.

Title: Demotic Egyptian Dialogues of Thoth
Content Summary: Fragments of dialogues between Thoth and a disciple on wisdom teachings.
Influence: Direct precursor to Greek Hermetic dialogues.

Title: Chaldean Oracles
Content Summary: Mystical verses blending Platonic philosophy with Eastern mysticism.
Influence: Influenced later Neoplatonism and Hermeticism.

Title: The Book of Breathings
Content Summary: Esoteric text on spiritual transformation
Influence: Influenced concepts of spiritual ascent in Hermeticism

Title: Greek Magical Papyri (PGM)
Content Summary: A collection of magical spells, invocations, and rituals, some attributed to Hermes Trismegistus.
Influence: Influenced Greco-Egyptian occultism and later Hermetic magic.

Title: Stobaean Fragments
Content Summary: Fragments of Hermetic wisdom compiled by John Stobaeus. Discusses the soul’s journey and its divine connection.
Influence: Preserved lost Hermetic texts that influenced Neoplatonism.

Title: Poimandres (CH 1) (Corpus Hermeticum)
Content Summary: A visionary dialogue where Hermes receives divine revelation about the cosmos and human salvation.
Influence: Central to later Hermetic and Gnostic thought.

Title: Corpus Hermeticum (Various Treatises)
Content Summary: A collection of 17 dialogues on divine wisdom, creation, and spiritual transformation.
Influence: Influenced Neoplatonism, Gnosticism, and Christian mysticism.

Title: The Discourse on the Eighth and Ninth (CH 6-7)
Content Summary: Mystical instruction on transcending material existence to attain divine union.
Influence: Influenced theurgy and later Gnostic thought.

Title: The Perfect Sermon (Asclepius)
Content Summary: Discusses divine wisdom, prophecy, the nature of gods, and the fate of Egypt.
Influence: Important in early Christian and Gnostic traditions.

Title: Definitions of Hermes Trismegistus to Asclepius
Content Summary: Short aphorisms outlining Hermetic teachings on the soul and cosmos.
Influence: Quoted by later Neoplatonists.

Title: The Emerald Tablet
Content Summary: A cryptic alchemical text linking macrocosm and microcosm.
Influence: Foundation of medieval and Renaissance alchemy.

Title: Arabic Hermetica (Kitāb sirr al-khalīqa, Picatrix, etc.)
Content Summary: Hermetic teachings mixed with Islamic occult sciences, alchemy, and astrology.
Influence: Influenced Islamic and European esotericism.

Title: The Book of Hermes on the 15 Fixed Stars
Content Summary: Astrological treatise on celestial influences and talismanic magic.
Influence: Influenced medieval and Renaissance astrology.

Title: Sirr al-khalīqa wa-ṣanʿat al-ṭabīʿa (The Secret of Creation and the Art of Nature)
Content Summary: Pseudo-Aristotelian text on Hermetic teachings
Influence: Influenced later Arabic Hermetic traditions

Title: Epistles of the Ikhwān al-Ṣafāʾ (The Epistles of the Brethren of Purity)
Content Summary: Encyclopedic work incorporating Hermetic ideas
Influence: Spread Hermetic concepts in Islamic intellectual circles

Title: Ghāyat al-Ḥakīm (The Aim of the Sage, known as Picatrix in Latin)
Content Summary: Compilation of Hermetic magical and astrological knowledge
Influence: Major influence on Western esoteric traditions

Title: Liber Antimaquis
Content Summary: Translation of Arabic Hermetic magical text
Influence: Introduced Arabic Hermetic concepts to Latin readers

Title: Isis the Prophetess to Her Son Horus
Content Summary: Dialogue on cosmic mysteries and esoteric teachings
Influence: Influenced later Hermetic and alchemical traditions

Title: Demotic text with dialogues between Thoth and a disciple
Content Summary: Wisdom teachings in dialogue form
Influence: Precursor to Greek Hermetic dialogues
